title: Nova Locutio Encrypted Envelope
description: A confidentiality wrapper for a Nova Locutio payload (v0.2). A hybrid multi-recipient sealed box: the payload is encrypted once under a random content-encryption key (CEK), and the CEK is wrapped for each recipient via X25519 ECDH against the recipient's did:nova key. The envelope is a transport artifact, not a commons artifact: it is NOT content-addressed and carries no hash of its own. Confidentiality is layered over — not instead of — signing: the cleartext payload is normally a fully-signed inner message, so decrypting yields verifiable provenance. See spec/encryption.md.

enc.description: AEAD used for the payload and for key wrapping. v0.2 fixes XChaCha20-Poly1305 (24-byte nonce, 16-byte tag). Closed enum; new algorithms require a version bump.

kdf.description: KDF deriving the per-recipient key-encryption key from the ECDH shared secret. v0.2 fixes HKDF-SHA-256 (RFC 5869).

kex.description: Key agreement: X25519 ECDH with public keys obtained by mapping each party's did:nova Ed25519 key to Curve25519 (the standard birational map). No key material beyond the signing DID is required.

epk.description: Sender's ephemeral X25519 public key (32 bytes, base64). Fresh per envelope, giving forward secrecy for the key-wrap step.

nonce.description: XChaCha20-Poly1305 nonce for the payload (24 bytes, base64).

ciphertext.description: AEAD ciphertext of the payload, with the 16-byte Poly1305 tag appended (base64). Bound to 'aad' if present.

recipients.description: One entry per recipient. Each carries the CEK wrapped to that recipient. The list is plaintext metadata: who can read the envelope is not itself hidden.

aad.description: Optional additional authenticated data bound to the payload ciphertext (e.g. a routing label or the hash of the inner message). Not encrypted; authenticated.

addressing.description: Recipient-addressing mode (v0.3, spec/encryption.md). Absent or 'direct': each recipient entry carries its cleartext 'to' DID (the recipient set is public metadata). 'stealth': recipient DIDs are omitted and the per-recipient wrap is bound to a fixed label, so a recipient recovers the key by trial-decryption — hiding who can read the envelope. The crypto construction is otherwise identical, so the envelope format version 'v' stays '0.2'.

then.description: Stealth: no recipient entry may carry a cleartext 'to' DID.

else.description: Direct (default): every recipient entry must carry its 'to' DID.

to.description: Recipient DID (direct addressing only; omitted under stealth). v0.2 key agreement requires a did:nova:<64-hex> Ed25519 key; the field accepts any DID syntactically.

wrap_nonce.description: XChaCha20-Poly1305 nonce for wrapping the CEK to this recipient (24 bytes, base64).

wrapped_key.description: The 32-byte CEK encrypted under this recipient's key-encryption key, with the 16-byte tag appended (base64). AAD is the recipient DID, binding the wrap to the recipient.

b64_24.description: Base64 of exactly 24 bytes (32 base64 chars, no padding needed since 24 is divisible by 3).

b64_32.description: Base64 of exactly 32 bytes (44 chars including one '=' pad).
